drive._units_note: All speeds mm/s; motor_deadband is mm/s (stakeholder 2026-07-20): the smallest wheel speed that actually moves the plant, = outputDeadband(0.03 duty) / vel_kff(0.002) = 15 mm/s. Firmware/host convert to duty as needed; no code default -- config is the source of truth.

control._vel_gains_domain: duty [-1,1] (NezhaMotor MotorVelocityPid); NOT the old PWM-percent scale
control._neutral_note: NEUTRAL baseline (stakeholder 2026-07-18): this is the no-calibration profile, so the gains are the most vanilla usable set, not a tuning. vel_kp = 0.002 = 1/500: full duty at the plant's ~500 mm/s full-scale speed (duty = error / v_max, the plainest proportional normalization; a pure-P loop droops by design). vel_kff = 0.002 = 1/500 too -- the SAME vanilla inverse-plant slope, and the open-loop law itself: with the PID disabled (TestGUI PID checkbox) the motor drives duty = kff * velocityTarget, so kff=0 would make PID-off dead rather than open-loop. vel_ki = 0 (no integrator), vel_imax/vel_kaw = 0 (inert with ki=0; kaw=0 = anti-windup off). vel_filt = 1.0 (no EMA smoothing, raw measured velocity; NOTE: no live SET key -- build-time bake only). This config is the source of truth for the drive/control parameters -- there are to be NO tunable defaults in the source. (The former UPDATE 2026-07-20 sentence here, about a half-dozen sibling control.* heading/distance/model-tau keys, is moot -- those keys were deleted, 119-003 config-attic sweep, no living consumer survived 115-003's motion-stack excision.)
control._sprint_114_note: a_max/a_decel/v_body_max/j_max/yaw_jerk_max added 2026-07-20 (sprint 114 ticket 002, config-as-truth completion): gen_boot_config.py no longer carries these as Python-side *_DEFAULT fallback constants -- every value below is seeded with the exact former default, value-preserving (verified byte-identical boot_config.cpp regen). This note originally also covered a half-dozen sibling control.* heading/lead keys -- ALL deleted (119-003, config-attic sweep): no living consumer survived 115-003's motion-stack excision.
control._shaper_note: decel-into-the-goal campaign (2026-07-22): a_max/a_decel/j_max/yaw_jerk_max read again by gen_boot_config.py's shaper_config_for_config() (Motion::VelocityShaper) -- see tovez.json's own _shaper_note for the full history/derivation. alpha_max/alpha_decel are new this campaign (a_max/a_decel's angular sibling); 7.0rad/s^2 matches tovez.json's own value/derivation (taper ~16.4deg from omega=2rad/s). j_max/yaw_jerk_max power the jerk-limited S-curve stage (same day) -- values unchanged, carried forward from their pre-existing sprint-114 bake.

estimator._estimator_note: 117 (predict-to-now estimator v1): weight_heading_otos/weight_omega_otos committed 0.0 -- stakeholder's encoder-only-v1 decision, OTOS fusion is wired but not yet trusted with any nonzero weight. staleness_ms=60.0 is 3x Devices::Otos::kReadPeriod (20ms): generous headroom over the OTOS chip's own read cadence, small enough to exclude a genuinely stale reading before the next cycle's fresh one arrives. (118 ticket 004, land-at-zero-completion-delete-stop-lead.md: this section's own former fourth key -- a boot-time anticipation-lead constant with a four-retune history and no stable value -- is DELETED; the mechanism it fed no longer exists, see App::MoveQueue::tick()'s own doc comment for the land-at-zero completion predicate that replaces it.)
